behavioral_changes.summary: Refresh-trigger template rendering now carries `request.MlvExecutionDefinitionId` through the renderer model as `mlv_exec_defn_id` and emits that value into the generated trigger-definition parameter payload.
`FMLVActivatorParser` now extracts `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` from nested `FabricItemInvocation` parameters and populates `action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId` when the extracted value is a valid GUID.

`FindFmlvExecutionDefinitionId` now defends against missing trigger-definition structure and malformed parameter groups by returning null or skipping bad groups instead of dereferencing absent tokens.
The refresh notebook now special-cases the production API host and calls the path-based `RefreshMaterializedLakeViews` job-instances endpoint.
The refresh notebook now sends `executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId` as JSON only when a non-empty execution-definition id is present, while preserving a bodyless POST when the id is blank.
The refresh notebook now fails fast on unsuccessful HTTP responses and no longer assumes a `Location` response header is always present.

grounding_evidence.reason: The adapter now threads an optional MLV execution-definition id into the template-rendering request, defaulting missing values to an empty string.
The XML-doc contract adds a new supported renderer input for template generation.
The renderer constructor now accepts and stores the new execution-definition value on the template model.
The template model now exposes a new Scriban-visible property named `mlv_exec_defn_id`.
Generated trigger definitions now serialize the execution-definition id into the parameter value slot that previously emitted `trigger_id`.
Parsing now hydrates the action with a valid execution-definition GUID and explicitly guards assignment behind `Guid.TryParse`.
New helper logic walks the nested trigger-definition structure to find `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId`, while short-circuiting or skipping malformed nodes.
The notebook template now declares an optional runtime input for an execution-definition id.
The notebook now chooses a bare production host for `prod` and keeps env-prefixed hosts for other environments.
The refresh submission URI now uses the path-based jobs endpoint instead of the previous query-string form.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
"exec_def_id = str(fmlvExecutionDefinitionId).strip() if fmlvExecutionDefinitionId else ''
",
"if exec_def_id:
",
"    headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
",
"    body = {"executionData": {"mlvExecutionDefinitionId": exec_def_id}}
",
"    resp = requests.post(publicuri, headers=headers, data=json.dumps(body))
",
"else:
",
"    resp = requests.post(publicuri, headers=headers)
",
grounding_evidence.reason: The notebook now branches on a non-empty execution-definition id to send a JSON body; blank or whitespace input keeps the old bodyless POST behavior.
Failed refresh-submission responses now surface as errors instead of proceeding as though submission succeeded.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
"if 'Location' in resp.headers:
",
"    mlvRefreshStatusUrl = resp.headers['Location']
",
"    print(f"FMLV refresh successfully submitted. Status url: {mlvRefreshStatusUrl}")
",
grounding_evidence.reason: Status-url logging is now conditional, avoiding a hard dependency on the response including a `Location` header.

scenario_sketches.title: Render `mlv_exec_defn_id` into generated trigger-definition parameters
scenario_sketches.rationale: Verify that a request carrying `MlvExecutionDefinitionId` flows through the renderer model and appears in the generated trigger definition where the FabricItemParameter value is emitted.

scenario_sketches.title: Populate `action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId` from a valid nested parameter value
scenario_sketches.rationale: A trigger definition containing a valid `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId` in a `FabricItemInvocation` parameter group should round-trip into the parsed action model as a GUID.

scenario_sketches.title: Ignore extracted execution-definition strings that are not valid GUIDs
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new `Guid.TryParse` gate is a separate guard: malformed execution-definition strings should not populate `action.FmlvExecutionDefinitionId`.

scenario_sketches.title: Return null when the execution-definition parameter path is absent
scenario_sketches.rationale: Missing `steps` / `rows` / `parameters.values` structure should short-circuit to null rather than throwing while searching for `fmlvExecutionDefinitionId`.

scenario_sketches.title: Skip malformed parameter groups that lack usable arguments or parameter names
scenario_sketches.rationale: The helper now continues past parameter groups whose `arguments` collection is missing or whose `parameterName` cannot be read, so malformed groups should not crash parsing.

scenario_sketches.title: Return null when the matching execution-definition group has no parameterValue payload
scenario_sketches.rationale: Even after a matching `parameterName`, missing `parameterValue.values[0].value` should resolve to null instead of dereferencing absent JSON tokens.

scenario_sketches.title: Resolve `API_HOST` to the bare production host only when `ENV == "prod"`
scenario_sketches.rationale: The new host-selection branch introduces a boundary case for production versus non-production environments that should be locked down explicitly.

scenario_sketches.title: Call the path-based `RefreshMaterializedLakeViews` instances endpoint
scenario_sketches.rationale: The refresh notebook now targets a different public URI shape, so submission should be validated against the new path-based endpoint contract.

scenario_sketches.title: POST `executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId` JSON when an execution-definition id is provided
scenario_sketches.rationale: Multi-schedule support depends on the notebook converting a non-empty execution-definition id into a JSON request body with the expected content type and field name.

scenario_sketches.title: Keep the refresh POST body empty when the execution-definition id is blank or whitespace
scenario_sketches.rationale: Blank or whitespace execution-definition values follow a distinct branch that should preserve the previous bodyless POST behavior.

scenario_sketches.title: Raise on failed refresh-submission HTTP responses
scenario_sketches.rationale: `resp.raise_for_status()` adds a new observable failure mode for non-success responses and should be exercised directly.

scenario_sketches.title: Do not require a `Location` response header before logging the status URL
scenario_sketches.rationale: The notebook now treats `Location` as optional, so a successful response without that header should not fail during status logging.
